__fixture__.name: restricted_managed_only_policy
__fixture__.scenario: Restricted-managed-only boundary under managed_workspace identity mode. An org policy narrows one capability surface (managed AI quota view) to managed-sign-in-required while leaving the rest of the install running under the managed convenience layer. Local workspace, local Git, local tasks, and BYOK AI stay usable. The downgrade path names policy_narrowed and preserves local work.

boundary_label: Managed sign-in required for the managed AI quota surface
plain_language_summary: Your org policy requires managed sign-in for the managed AI quota surface. Local workspace, local Git, local tasks, and BYOK AI continue without sign-in.

visible_downgrade_path.downgrade_note: If the managed sign-in is declined or blocked, the managed AI quota surface narrows to local-only; other managed features remain available under the managed posture.

preserved_local_work.note: Local work is intact. The managed AI quota surface is managed-sign-in-required by org policy; other capability stays available under the managed posture.
retained_capabilities: Open, edit, save, and undo local workspaces.
Search over the local workspace and navigate symbols.
Run local Git commands and commit locally.
Run local tasks and debuggers in trusted workspaces.
Use BYOK AI providers and local model execution.
blocked_capabilities: Open the managed AI quota surface until managed sign-in completes.

reserved_rows.note: Passkey / WebAuthn step-up is supported via the system browser for managed sign-in.
Reserved for the session-expiry / policy-epoch-roll reauth row applied to the managed AI quota surface.
Reserved for the managed seat-removal row; local-only work MUST remain representable.
Declares that managed deprovision of this restricted surface MUST preserve local workspace, local Git, local tasks, and BYOK AI capability.
